charts.title: Bar (vertical)
charts.ariaLabel: Vertical bar chart example showing visits by channel.

charts.title: Bar (horizontal)
charts.ariaLabel: Horizontal bar chart example showing requests by region.

details.useWhen: Labels are long or showing a ranked list.

charts.title: Stacked bar or column
charts.ariaLabel: Stacked bar chart example showing completion status by program.

details.avoidWhen: Precise comparison of segments is required.
details.notes: Label clearly and limit number of segments.

charts.title: Grouped horizontal bar
charts.ariaLabel: Grouped horizontal bar chart comparing request counts by region across two years.

details.useWhen: Comparing multiple series across categories with long labels.
details.avoidWhen: Too many categories or series create visual clutter.
details.notes: Keep category count manageable and series colours clearly distinct.

charts.title: Line
charts.ariaLabel: Line chart example showing applications over time.

charts.title: Multi-line
charts.ariaLabel: Multi-line chart example showing two monthly trends over time.

details.useWhen: Comparing trends across multiple series over the same time period.
details.avoidWhen: Too many lines make patterns difficult to read.
details.notes: Keep line count low and label series clearly.

charts.title: Area
charts.ariaLabel: Area chart example showing total users over time.

details.useWhen: Showing cumulative volume or share over time.

charts.title: Line + bar
charts.ariaLabel: Combined line and bar chart showing application volume and conversion rate by month.

details.useWhen: Showing two related measures with different units over time.
details.avoidWhen: Combined series are unrelated or dual axes may mislead interpretation.
details.notes: Label both axes clearly and use sparingly.

charts.title: Stacked area
charts.ariaLabel: Stacked area chart example showing cumulative channel share over time.

details.useWhen: Showing how components contribute to a total over time.
details.avoidWhen: Users need precise comparison of individual series values.
details.notes: Keep series count low and order layers consistently.

charts.title: Doughnut
charts.ariaLabel: Doughnut chart example showing approval status with direct slice labels.

details.notes: Label slices directly with category and percentage; include total value nearby when relevant.

charts.title: Pie
charts.ariaLabel: Pie chart example showing channel share with direct slice labels.

details.notes: Prefer doughnut or bar chart. When you do use a pie chart, label slices directly.

charts.title: Stacked proportional bar (100%)
charts.ariaLabel: 100 percent stacked bar chart showing proportional outcome share by channel.

details.useWhen: Comparing composition where each category sums to 100%.
details.avoidWhen: Users need absolute totals rather than proportions.
details.notes: Label percentages clearly and provide totals separately when needed.

charts.title: Scatter plot
charts.ariaLabel: Scatter plot example showing relationship between two variables.

charts.title: Bubble plot
charts.ariaLabel: Bubble plot example showing demand and wait time across locations with bubble size as volume.

details.useWhen: Showing relationship and magnitude across two variables.
details.avoidWhen: Bubble area is the only cue and labels are missing.
details.notes: Keep bubble sizes distinct and provide labels or table support.

charts.title: Vertical dot plot
charts.ariaLabel: Vertical dot plot example showing distribution of wait times by weekday.

details.useWhen: Showing distributions across categories without bars.
details.notes: Use jitter, labels, or supporting summaries for readability.

charts.title: Histogram
charts.ariaLabel: Histogram example showing distribution of processing times in binned ranges.

details.useWhen: Showing frequency distribution across numeric ranges.
details.avoidWhen: Exact values per individual record are required.
details.notes: Use sensible bin widths and label ranges clearly.

charts.title: Radar
charts.ariaLabel: Radar chart example comparing capability scores across dimensions.

details.useWhen: Comparing profiles across the same fixed dimensions.
details.avoidWhen: Precise value comparison is the primary goal.
details.notes: Limit categories and provide supporting values in text or table.
